title: Strongly Invertible Knots, Khovanov Homotopy, and Localization
speaker: Robert Lipshitz
abstract: Strong inversions are a class of order-2 symmetries of knots in . Building on work of Seidel-Smith, Lidman-Manolescu, Stoffregen-Zhang, and others, we will describe a relationship between the Khovanov homology of a knot with a strong inversion and its quotients by the inversion. We will also give a modest application to surfaces in 4-space. This is joint work with Sucharit Sarkar. While there is no symplectic geometry in the talk, many of the ideas come from or may be useful in Floer-theoretic settings.

proof_stages.label: strong inversion and quotient data
proof_stages.summary: The talk sets up strongly invertible knots as knots preserved by a half-turn reversing the knot orientation, explains transversion and introversion diagrams, and describes the quotient/annular quotient obtained after resolving the axis intersections.

proof_stages.label: combinatorial Khovanov homology
proof_stages.summary: Khovanov homology is recalled as a bigraded homology theory built from a knot diagram by the cube of resolutions, the Frobenius algebra Z[x]/(x^2), and a total chain complex.

proof_stages.label: symplectic Khovanov motivation
proof_stages.summary: The Seidel-Smith style symplectic model is reviewed via a Lefschetz fibration, Hilbert schemes, Lagrangian products, and known comparison/localization results for symplectic Khovanov homology of periodic knots.

proof_stages.label: localization theorem for strongly invertible knots
proof_stages.summary: The main theorem asserts a spectral sequence from Khovanov homology over F_2 of a strongly invertible knot to an annular Khovanov-type homology of the quotient, with first differential id plus the involution-induced map and controlled gradings.

proof_stages.label: obstruction to equivariant slice disks
proof_stages.summary: Combining the spectral sequence, the induced involution on Khovanov homology, and nontrivial Khovanov cobordism maps for slice disks yields an obstruction showing that 9_46 has no equivariant slice disk.

proof_stages.label: Smith-theoretic proof mechanism
proof_stages.summary: Classical P. A. Smith theory is recast as a bicomplex spectral sequence from H_*(X) to H_*(X^tau), then applied to the Khovanov stable homotopy type, with the main work being identification of the fixed set as the annular quotient theory.

proof_stages.label: related localization results and open directions
proof_stages.summary: The result is placed among Seidel-Smith Lagrangian Floer localization, periodic-knot and Heegaard Floer analogues, symplectic Khovanov homotopy types, and possible future applications to freely periodic knots and purely symplectic questions.
